summary: Camera-focused decomp and header cleanup that renamed the camera subject allocation/list type from CameraBox to CmSubject across camera, fighter, item, ground, and map code; separated CameraDebugMode storage from the main Camera struct; added CameraType and camera bound bitmask names; and improved/matched several camera functions. The decomp.dev bot reported +2312 matched bytes, 4 new full matches in main/melee/cm/camera, and 2 unmatched-function improvements.
what_changed: Replaced CameraBox with CmSubject throughout camera subject APIs and consumers, including Camera_80028F5C, Camera_80029020, Camera_80029044, Camera_800290D4, Camera_8002928C, Camera_80030CD8, Camera_80030CFC, Fighter::x890_cameraBox, Item::x520_cameraBox, grStadium display data, ftLib_80086B74, and mpLib drawing code.
Documented that the CmSubject name was found at 80029070 and kept the subject struct layout with offset comments in src/melee/cm/types.h.
Added named camera boundary bit masks TOP_BOUND, BOTTOM_BOUND, LEFT_BOUND, and RIGHT_BOUND, replacing raw constants 1, 2, 4, and 8 in Camera_80029124 and downstream checks.

Added CameraType enum values for normal gameplay, pause, training menu, clear, fixed, free, boss intro, debug follow, and debug free camera modes, and changed Camera::mode and CameraDebugMode::last_mode to CameraType.

Moved CameraDebugMode out of struct Camera into separate static/global storage cm_80453004, changed Camera size assertion to 0x39C, and updated debug camera code in dbcamera.c to use follow_* and free_* field names instead of mode7_* and mode8_*.

Refactored Camera_8002958C variable names and logic around subject counting, tracking weights, boundary clamping, min/max bounds, default stage offset, and z factor while preserving matching-sensitive structure.
Introduced small matching-oriented inline helpers including get_follow_speed, get_delta, get_max_bounds_length, and get_stage_floor_height.

Implemented previously stubbed or unmatched camera routines Camera_8002DDC4 and Camera_8002DFE4, and converted Camera_8002DFE4's prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 Camera_8002DFE4(Vec3*, Vec3*, CameraTransformState*).
Reworked Camera_80029AAC and Camera_80029CF8 with clearer names for spread, follow speed, FOV halves, bounds length, pitch/pan calculations, and HSD_ASSERTMSG assertions.
Changed Camera_80029124 return type from u32 to s32 and parameter name from arg0 to subject_pos, with casts where existing comparisons expected unsigned behavior.
smart_moves: Renaming CameraBox to CmSubject propagated the recovered symbol name project-wide instead of leaving semantic drift between camera internals and fighter/item/ground users.
Separating CameraDebugMode from Camera resolved the Camera struct size expectation: the diff removed debug_mode from Camera and added STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Camera) == 0x39C).
Replacing raw bound flags with named LEFT_BOUND, RIGHT_BOUND, TOP_BOUND, and BOTTOM_BOUND made boundary tests searchable and reduced the risk of mixing up bit meanings during later camera decomp.
Using CameraType enum names turned switch cases in Camera_8002A4AC from numeric modes into readable mode-specific branches without changing the apparent behavior.
Extracting repeated interpolation and bounds-size formulas into inline helpers made matching-sensitive math reusable while preserving the decompilation's required instruction shape.

The allocation/free-list setup in Camera_80028B9C was tightened to iterate to n_subjects - 1 and set cm_804D645C[i].prev = NULL, avoiding the older loop pattern that wrote the terminal link inside an all-subject loop.
decomp_lessons: When an in-game debug string or nearby code reveals a real struct name, update forward declarations, struct definitions, globals, and every consumer type together; partial renames leave confusing API seams.
Camera state may contain adjacent globals that were mistakenly embedded in a parent struct. A static size assertion is useful evidence for splitting storage back out.
Named bit flags are especially valuable for matching camera boundary code because the same flag result feeds many repeated clamping blocks.
For camera math, descriptive temporary names such as base_pos, test_pos, tracking_multiplier, min_x, max_y, fov_u, fov_d, fov_r, and fov_l make later equivalence checks easier than stack-slot names.
Small inline helpers can be a matching tactic, not just style cleanup; this PR explicitly notes matching oddities around get_follow_speed taking globals->x38 as a parameter.
If signedness changes are needed for matching or prototypes, keep explicit casts at call sites where previous comparisons depended on unsigned semantics.
Use project assertion macros such as HSD_ASSERTMSG when replacing hand-written __assert patterns in matched camera code.
naming_conventions: Camera subject list nodes should use CmSubject, not CameraBox, when referring to the struct allocated by Camera_80028B9C/Camera_80029044.
Camera bounds subject count field was renamed from subjects to total_subjects.
CameraDebugMode mode7_* fields were renamed to follow_* for CAMERA_DEBUG_FOLLOW behavior.
CameraDebugMode mode8_* fields were renamed to free_* for CAMERA_DEBUG_FREE behavior.
Camera mode integer constants should use CameraType names such as CAMERA_STANDARD, CAMERA_PAUSE, CAMERA_FIXED, CAMERA_FREE, CAMERA_DEBUG_FOLLOW, and CAMERA_DEBUG_FREE.
Camera boundary bit checks should use TOP_BOUND, BOTTOM_BOUND, LEFT_BOUND, and RIGHT_BOUND rather than raw literals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Camera_80029124 retained an explicit u8 _PAD[4] with a todo noting there are many ways to match without the padding, indicating stack-shape sensitivity.
Camera_80029044 replaced a labeled self-goto infinite loop with while (true) {} after OSReport("couldn't get CmSubject struct.\n", arg0).
Camera_80029AAC used inline get_follow_speed and get_delta helpers to preserve the interpolation behavior and matching shape.
Camera_80029CF8 used get_max_bounds_length and HSD_ASSERTMSG for cleaner matching around FOV/frustum calculations.
F32_MAX and -F32_MAX replaced explicit 3.4028235e38f sentinel values in bounds initialization and stage-floor helper code.
Camera_8002DFE4 was recovered as a typed function returning s32 and interpolating interest based on cm_80452C68.x341_b5_b6 and x378/x37C state.
The bot report showed new full matches for Camera_8002DFE4, Camera_80028B9C, Camera_8002A4AC, and Camera_80029AAC, plus improvements for Camera_8002DDC4 and Camera_80029CF8.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback for GALE01 reported matched code increasing to 47.19% (+0.06%, +2312 bytes).

Automated report listed 4 new matches: Camera_8002DFE4 (+372 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%), Camera_80028B9C (+12 bytes, 98.80% to 100.00%), Camera_8002A4AC (+12 bytes, 98.36% to 100.00%), and Camera_80029AAC (+2 bytes, 99.36% to 100.00%).
Automated report listed 2 improvements: Camera_8002DDC4 (+339 bytes, 0.00% to 62.40%) and Camera_80029CF8 (+25 bytes, 76.29% to 78.90%).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching CmSubject field names x10, x1C, x2C, x34, x40, x48, x54, and x60.
Search for remaining CameraBox references after PR 1984 to verify the rename was completed everywhere.
Inspect subsequent camera.c PRs for cleanup of the Camera_80029124 padding todo.
Look for later work on Camera_8002DDC4 and Camera_80029CF8 to see whether the partial matches from this PR were completed.
Search whether CameraDebugMode cm_80453004 linkage between camera.static.h and dbcamera.c was later adjusted or documented.
Check later PRs for replacement of remaining numeric camera mode checks with CameraType enum names.
